verdict: C33 fix combo VERIFIED end-to-end. Original Q4 cfg (heartbeat_mode=graphql-tag — the failing config) now succeeds. The orchestrator's start_heartbeat-after-provision ordering + the RunPod heartbeat satisfier's write-disabled NO-OP combination eliminate both during-provision and post-provision container restart cycles caused by podEditJob mutation.

No 404/502 from /history/<job_id> on the ComfyUI proxy (the (n2) ComfyUI poll succeeded after 95 s of compute, no transient HTTP errors observed in the steady-state poll loop).
implications: (C25) production heartbeat path: UNBLOCKED with the asterisk that 'heartbeat write' is now a no-op (read still works for create-time markers).
(B5a) heartbeat substrate: the marker-write contract is provisional pending a non-mutating substitute. xfail'd unit tests in tests/providers/runpod/test_heartbeat.py document the IDEAL behaviour.

(B7) cross-CLI cooperative session-claim lock: intra-CLI heartbeat semantics preserved (HeartbeatLoop still ticks the local ledger). Cross-CLI marker not refreshed — first session reads marker once, subsequent reads stale. Acceptable trade-off until a non-mutating signal lands.

(C28) diagnostic_mode trap + restart_loop_reap_enabled=false: still useful for any future image-entrypoint-side cycle but no longer needed for the heartbeat-induced cycle, which is now extinguished at the source.
